catalog.title: Australian Information Security Manual (ISM)
catalog.description:
This publication provides a catalog of security and privacy controls for information systems and organizations to protect organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, and the Nation from a diverse set of threats and risks, including hostile attacks, human errors, natural disasters, structural failures, foreign intelligence entities, and privacy risks.

catalog.abstract: This publication provides a catalog of security and privacy controls for federal information systems and organizations and a process for selecting controls to protect organizational operations (including mission, functions, image, and reputation), organizational assets, individuals, other organizations, and the Nation from a diverse set of threats including hostile cyber attacks, natural disasters, structural failures, and human errors (both intentional and unintentional). The security and privacy controls are customizable and implemented as part of an organization-wide process that manages information security and privacy risk. The controls address a diverse set of security and privacy requirements across the federal government and critical infrastructure, derived from legislation, Executive Orders, policies, directives, regulations, standards, and/or mission/business needs. The publication also describes how to develop specialized sets of controls, or overlays, tailored for specific types of missions/business functions, technologies, or environments of operation. Finally, the catalog of security controls addresses security from both a functionality perspective (the strength of security functions and mechanisms provided) and an assurance perspective (the measures of confidence in the implemented security capability). Addressing both security functionality and assurance helps to ensure that information technology component products and the information systems built from those products using sound system and security engineering principles are sufficiently trustworthy.
catalog.keywords: FIPS Publication 200; FISMA; Privacy Act; Risk Management Framework; security controls; FIPS Publication 199; security requirements; computer security; assurance;

securityControls.title: ism-principle-g1 - Security Principle: G1
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-g1_smt}} - A Chief Information Security Officer provides leadership and oversight of cyber security.

securityControls.title: ism-0039 - Control: ism-0039
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0039_smt}} - A cyber security strategy is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1104 - Control: ism-1104
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1104_smt}} - Wall outlet boxes have connectors on opposite sides of the wall outlet box if the cable group contains cables belonging to different systems.

securityControls.title: ism-0292 - Control: ism-0292
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0292_smt}} - High assurance ICT equipment is always operated in an evaluated configuration.

securityControls.title: ism-1655 - Control: ism-1655
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1655_smt}} - .NET Framework 3.5 (includes .NET 2.0 and 3.0) is disabled or removed.

securityControls.title: ism-0418 - Control: ism-0418
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0418_smt}} - Credentials are kept separate from systems they are used to authenticate to, except for when performing authentication activities.

securityControls.title: ism-1817 - Control: ism-1817
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1817_smt}} - Clients are authenticated when calling web APIs that facilitate access to data not authorised for release into the public domain.

securityControls.title: ism-1320 - Control: ism-1320
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1320_smt}} - MAC address filtering is not used to restrict which devices can connect to wireless networks.

securityControls.title: ism-1192 - Control: ism-1192
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1192_smt}} - Gateways inspect and filter data flows at the transport and above network layers.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p2 - Security Principle: P2
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p2_smt}} - Systems and applications are delivered and supported by trusted suppliers.

securityControls.title: ism-0731 - Control: ism-0731
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0731_smt}} - The CISO oversees cyber supply chain risk management activities for their organisation.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-r1 - Security Principle: R1
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-r1_smt}} - Cyber security incidents are reported both internally and externally to relevant bodies in a timely manner.

securityControls.title: ism-0043 - Control: ism-0043
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0043_smt}} - Systems have an incident response plan that covers the following: * guidelines on what constitutes a cyber security incident * the types of cyber security incidents likely to be encountered and the expected response to each type * how to report cyber security incidents, internally to an organisation and externally to relevant authorities * other parties which need to be informed in the event of a cyber security incident * the authority, or authorities, responsible for investigating and responding to cyber security incidents * the criteria by which an investigation of a cyber security incident would be requested from a law enforcement agency, the Australian Cyber Security Centre or other relevant authority * the steps necessary to ensure the integrity of evidence relating to a cyber security incident * system contingency measures or a reference to such details if they are located in a separate document.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-g2 - Security Principle: G2
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-g2_smt}} - The identity and value of systems, applications and data is determined and documented.

securityControls.title: ism-0420 - Control: ism-0420
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0420_smt}} - Where a system processes, stores or communicates AUSTEO, AGAO or REL data, personnel who are foreign nationals are identified as such, including by their specific nationality.

securityControls.title: ism-0252 - Control: ism-0252
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0252_smt}} - Cyber security awareness training is undertaken annually by all personnel and covers: * the purpose of the cyber security awareness training * security appointments and contacts * authorised use of systems and their resources * protection of systems and their resources * reporting of cyber security incidents and suspected compromises of systems and their resources.

securityControls.title: ism-1109 - Control: ism-1109
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1109_smt}} - Wall outlet box covers are clear plastic.

securityControls.title: ism-0047 - Control: ism-0047
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0047_smt}} - Organisational-level security documentation is approved by the Chief Information Security Officer while system-specific security documentation is approved by the system’s authorising officer.

securityControls.title: ism-0546 - Control: ism-0546
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0546_smt}} - When video conferencing or IP telephony traffic passes through a gateway containing a firewall or proxy, a video-aware or voice-aware firewall or proxy is used.

securityControls.title: ism-1599 - Control: ism-1599
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1599_smt}} - ICT equipment is handled in a manner suitable for its sensitivity or classification.

securityControls.title: ism-1105 - Control: ism-1105
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1105_smt}} - Different cables groups do not share a wall outlet box.

securityControls.title: ism-1549 - Control: ism-1549
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1549_smt}} - A media management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1137 - Control: ism-1137
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1137_smt}} - System owners deploying SECRET or TOP SECRET systems in shared facilities contact the ACSC for an emanation security threat assessment and implement any additional installation criteria derived from the threat assessment.

securityControls.title: ism-1551 - Control: ism-1551
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1551_smt}} - An ICT equipment management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-0382 - Control: ism-0382
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0382_smt}} - Unprivileged users do not have the ability to uninstall or disable approved software.

securityControls.title: ism-1219 - Control: ism-1219
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1219_smt}} - MFD print drums and image transfer rollers are inspected and destroyed if there is remnant toner which cannot be removed or a print is visible on the image transfer roller.

securityControls.title: ism-0343 - Control: ism-0343
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0343_smt}} - If there is no business requirement for writing to removable media and devices, such functionality is disabled via the use of device access control software or by disabling external communication interfaces.

securityControls.title: ism-0853 - Control: ism-0853
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0853_smt}} - On a daily basis, outside of business hours and after an appropriate period of inactivity, user sessions are terminated and workstations are restarted.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p5 - Security Principle: P5
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p5_smt}} - Security vulnerabilities in systems and applications are identified and mitigated in a timely manner.

securityControls.title: ism-1492 - Control: ism-1492
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1492_smt}} - Operating system exploit protection functionality is enabled.

securityControls.title: ism-1661 - Control: ism-1661
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1661_smt}} - Allowed and blocked execution events on internet-facing servers are logged.

securityControls.title: ism-1699 - Control: ism-1699
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1699_smt}} - A vulnerability scanner is used at least weekly to identify missing patches or updates for security vulnerabilities in office productivity suites, web browsers and their extensions, email clients, PDF software, and security products.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-r3 - Security Principle: R3
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-r3_smt}} - Business continuity and disaster recovery plans are enacted when required.

securityControls.title: ism-1757 - Control: ism-1757
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1757_smt}} - Web application event logs are stored centrally.

securityControls.title: ism-1597 - Control: ism-1597
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1597_smt}} - Credentials are obscured as they are entered into systems.

securityControls.title: ism-1689 - Control: ism-1689
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1689_smt}} - Privileged accounts (excluding local administrator accounts) cannot logon to unprivileged operating environments.

securityControls.title: ism-0264 - Control: ism-0264
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0264_smt}} - An email usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-0725 - Control: ism-0725
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0725_smt}} - The CISO coordinates cyber security and business alignment through a cyber security steering committee or advisory board, comprising of key cyber security and business executives, which meets formally and on a regular basis.

securityControls.title: ism-1564 - Control: ism-1564
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1564_smt}} - At the conclusion of a security assessment for a system, a plan of action and milestones is produced by the system owner.

securityControls.title: ism-1324 - Control: ism-1324
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1324_smt}} - Certificates are generated using an evaluated certificate authority or hardware security module.

securityControls.title: ism-1818 - Control: ism-1818
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1818_smt}} - Clients are authenticated when calling web APIs that facilitate modification of data.

securityControls.title: ism-1740 - Control: ism-1740
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1740_smt}} - Personnel dealing with banking details and payment requests are advised of what business email compromise is, how to manage such situations and how to report it.

securityControls.title: ism-0460 - Control: ism-0460
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0460_smt}} - ASD-approved HACE is used when encrypting media that contains SECRET or TOP SECRET data.

securityControls.title: ism-1101 - Control: ism-1101
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1101_smt}} - In TOP SECRET areas, cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets in server rooms or communications rooms are terminated as close as possible to the cabinet.

securityControls.title: ism-0824 - Control: ism-0824
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0824_smt}} - Personnel are advised not to send or receive files via unauthorised online services.

securityControls.title: ism-1261 - Control: ism-1261
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1261_smt}} - Database administrator accounts are not shared across different databases.

securityControls.title: ism-0616 - Control: ism-0616
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0616_smt}} - Separation of duties is implemented in performing administrative activities for gateways.

securityControls.title: ism-1319 - Control: ism-1319
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1319_smt}} - Static addressing is not used for assigning IP addresses on wireless networks.

securityControls.title: ism-0246 - Control: ism-0246
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0246_smt}} - An emanation security threat assessment is sought as early as possible in a system’s life cycle as implementing emanation security can have significant cost implications.

securityControls.title: ism-1777 - Control: ism-1777
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1777_smt}} - Web proxy event logs are stored centrally.

securityControls.title: ism-0231 - Control: ism-0231
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0231_smt}} - When using cryptographic equipment to permit different levels of conversation for different kinds of connections, telephone systems give a visual indication of what kind of connection has been made.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p9 - Security Principle: P9
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p9_smt}} - Data, applications and configuration settings are backed up in a secure and proven manner on a regular basis.

securityControls.title: ism-1427 - Control: ism-1427
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1427_smt}} - Gateways perform ingress traffic filtering to detect and prevent IP source address spoofing.

securityControls.title: ism-0307 - Control: ism-0307
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0307_smt}} - If an uncleared technician is used to undertake maintenance or repairs of ICT equipment, the ICT equipment and associated media is sanitised before maintenance or repair work is undertaken.

securityControls.title: ism-1581 - Control: ism-1581
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1581_smt}} - Continuous real-time monitoring of the availability of online services is performed.

securityControls.title: ism-1223 - Control: ism-1223
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1223_smt}} - Memory in network devices is sanitised using the following processes, in order of preference: * following device-specific guidance provided in evaluation documentation * following vendor sanitisation guidance * loading a dummy configuration file, performing a factory reset and then reinstalling firmware.

securityControls.title: ism-1221 - Control: ism-1221
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1221_smt}} - Printers and MFDs are checked to ensure no pages are trapped in the paper path due to a paper jam.

securityControls.title: ism-0813 - Control: ism-0813
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0813_smt}} - Server rooms, communications rooms, security containers and secure rooms are not left in unsecured states.

securityControls.title: ism-1663 - Control: ism-1663
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1663_smt}} - Application control event logs are stored centrally.

securityControls.title: ism-1664 - Control: ism-1664
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1664_smt}} - Blocked PowerShell script execution events are logged.

securityControls.title: ism-1521 - Control: ism-1521
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1521_smt}} - CDSs implement protocol breaks at each network layer.

securityControls.title: ism-1656 - Control: ism-1656
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1656_smt}} - Application control is implemented on non-internet-facing servers.

securityControls.title: ism-0213 - Control: ism-0213
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0213_smt}} - Different cable groups do not terminate on the same patch panel.

securityControls.title: ism-1750 - Control: ism-1750
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1750_smt}} - Administrative infrastructure for critical servers, high-value servers and regular servers is segregated from each other.

securityControls.title: ism-1143 - Control: ism-1143
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1143_smt}} - Patch management processes, and supporting patch management procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-0979 - Control: ism-0979
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0979_smt}} - Legal advice is sought on the exact wording of logon banners.

securityControls.title: ism-0313 - Control: ism-0313
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0313_smt}} - ICT equipment sanitisation processes, and supporting ICT equipment sanitisation procedures, are developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1264 - Control: ism-1264
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1264_smt}} - Database administrator access is restricted to defined roles rather than accounts with default administrative permissions or all permissions.

securityControls.title: ism-1268 - Control: ism-1268
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1268_smt}} - The need-to-know principle is enforced for database contents through the application of minimum privileges, database views and database roles.

securityControls.title: ism-1582 - Control: ism-1582
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1582_smt}} - Application control rulesets are validated on an annual or more frequent basis.

securityControls.title: ism-1270 - Control: ism-1270
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1270_smt}} - Database servers are placed on a different network segment to user workstations.

securityControls.title: ism-1439 - Control: ism-1439
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1439_smt}} - If using CDNs, disclosing the IP addresses of web servers under an organisation’s control (referred to as origin servers) is avoided and access to the origin servers is restricted to the CDNs and authorised management networks.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-d2 - Security Principle: D2
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-d2_smt}} - Cyber security events are analysed in a timely manner to identify cyber security incidents.

securityControls.title: ism-1436 - Control: ism-1436
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1436_smt}} - Critical online services are segregated from other online services that are more likely to be targeted.

securityControls.title: ism-1776 - Control: ism-1776
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1776_smt}} - CDS event logs are stored centrally.

securityControls.title: ism-1606 - Control: ism-1606
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1606_smt}} - When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server’s hardware, patches, updates or vendor mitigations for security vulnerabilities are applied to the isolation mechanism and underlying operating system in a timely manner.

securityControls.title: ism-1330 - Control: ism-1330
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1330_smt}} - The PMK caching period is not set to greater than 1440 minutes (24 hours).

securityControls.title: ism-0714 - Control: ism-0714
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0714_smt}} - A CISO is appointed to provide cyber security leadership and guidance for their organisation.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p1 - Security Principle: P1
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p1_smt}} - Systems and applications are designed, deployed, maintained and decommissioned according to their value and their confidentiality, integrity and availability requirements.

securityControls.title: ism-0643 - Control: ism-0643
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0643_smt}} - Evaluated diodes are used for controlling the data flow of unidirectional gateways between an organisation’s networks and public network infrastructure.

securityControls.title: ism-0161 - Control: ism-0161
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0161_smt}} - ICT equipment and media are secured when not in use.

securityControls.title: ism-0629 - Control: ism-0629
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0629_smt}} - For gateways between networks belonging to different security domains, any shared components are managed by system administrators for the higher security domain or by system administrators from a mutually-agreed third party.

securityControls.title: ism-1245 - Control: ism-1245
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1245_smt}} - All temporary installation files and logs are removed after DBMS software has been installed.

securityControls.title: ism-0041 - Control: ism-0041
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0041_smt}} - Systems have a system security plan that includes a description of the system and an annex that covers both applicable controls from this document and any additional controls that have been identified.

securityControls.title: ism-0817 - Control: ism-0817
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0817_smt}} - Personnel are advised of what suspicious contact via online services is and how to report it.

securityControls.title: ism-0247 - Control: ism-0247
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0247_smt}} - System owners deploying SECRET or TOP SECRET systems with Radio Frequency transmitters inside or co-located with their facility contact the ACSC for an emanation security threat assessment and implement any additional installation criteria derived from the threat assessment.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p3 - Security Principle: P3
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p3_smt}} - Systems and applications are configured to reduce their attack surface.

securityControls.title: ism-1335 - Control: ism-1335
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1335_smt}} - Wireless access points enable the use of the 802.11w amendment to protect management frames.

securityControls.title: ism-1721 - Control: ism-1721
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1721_smt}} - TOP SECRET wall outlet boxes are coloured red.

securityControls.title: ism-0250 - Control: ism-0250
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0250_smt}} - ICT equipment meets industry and government standards relating to electromagnetic interference/electromagnetic compatibility.

securityControls.title: ism-0317 - Control: ism-0317
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0317_smt}} - At least three pages of random text with no blank areas are printed on each colour printer cartridge or MFD print drum.

securityControls.title: ism-0634 - Control: ism-0634
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0634_smt}} - The following events are logged for gateways: * data packets and data flows permitted through gateways * data packets and data flows attempting to leave gateways * real-time alerts for attempted intrusions.

securityControls.title: ism-1163 - Control: ism-1163
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1163_smt}} - Systems have a continuous monitoring plan that includes: * conducting vulnerability scans for systems at least monthly * conducting vulnerability assessments or penetration tests for systems at least annually * analysing identified security vulnerabilities to determine their potential impact * using a risk-based approach to prioritise the implementation of mitigations based on effectiveness and cost.

securityControls.title: ism-0293 - Control: ism-0293
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0293_smt}} - ICT equipment is classified based on the highest sensitivity or classification of data that it is approved for processing, storing or communicating.

securityControls.title: ism-0318 - Control: ism-0318
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0318_smt}} - When unable to sanitise printer cartridges or MFD print drums, they are destroyed as per electrostatic memory devices.

securityControls.title: ism-1660 - Control: ism-1660
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1660_smt}} - Allowed and blocked execution events on workstations are logged.

securityControls.title: ism-0218 - Control: ism-0218
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0218_smt}} - If TOP SECRET fibre-optic fly leads exceeding five metres in length are used to connect wall outlet boxes to ICT equipment, they are run in a protective and easily inspected pathway that is clearly labelled at the ICT equipment end with the wall outlet box’s identifier.

securityControls.title: ism-1592 - Control: ism-1592
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1592_smt}} - Unprivileged users do not have the ability to install unapproved software.

securityControls.title: ism-1621 - Control: ism-1621
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1621_smt}} - Windows PowerShell 2.0 is disabled or removed.

securityControls.title: ism-1571 - Control: ism-1571
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1571_smt}} - The right to verify compliance with security requirements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

securityControls.title: ism-1688 - Control: ism-1688
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1688_smt}} - Unprivileged accounts cannot logon to privileged operating environments.

securityControls.title: ism-1590 - Control: ism-1590
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1590_smt}} - Credentials are changed if: * they are directly compromised * they are suspected of being compromised * they appear in an online data breach database * they are discovered stored on networks in the clear * they are discovered being transferred across networks in the clear * membership of a shared account changes * they have not been changed in the past 12 months.

securityControls.title: ism-1079 - Control: ism-1079
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1079_smt}} - The ACSC’s approval is sought before undertaking any maintenance or repairs to high assurance ICT equipment.

securityControls.title: ism-1386 - Control: ism-1386
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1386_smt}} - Network management traffic can only originate from administrative infrastructure.

securityControls.title: ism-1119 - Control: ism-1119
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1119_smt}} - Cables in TOP SECRET areas are fully inspectable for their entire length.

securityControls.title: ism-1262 - Control: ism-1262
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1262_smt}} - Database administrators have unique and identifiable accounts.

securityControls.title: ism-1636 - Control: ism-1636
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1636_smt}} - System owners ensure controls for each system and its operating environment are assessed to determine if they have been implemented correctly and are operating as intended.

securityControls.title: ism-0843 - Control: ism-0843
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0843_smt}} - Application control is implemented on workstations.

securityControls.title: ism-1536 - Control: ism-1536
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1536_smt}} - The following events are logged for web applications: attempted access that is denied, crashes and error messages, and search queries initiated by users.

securityControls.title: ism-1243 - Control: ism-1243
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1243_smt}} - A database register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

securityControls.title: ism-1298 - Control: ism-1298
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1298_smt}} - Personnel are advised of privacy and security risks when travelling overseas with mobile devices.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-g4 - Security Principle: G4
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-g4_smt}} - Security risk management processes are embedded into organisational risk management frameworks.

securityControls.title: ism-0734 - Control: ism-0734
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0734_smt}} - The CISO contributes to the development and maintenance of business continuity and disaster recovery plans for their organisation to ensure that business-critical services are supported appropriately in the event of a disaster.

securityControls.title: ism-1441 - Control: ism-1441
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1441_smt}} - Where a requirement for high availability exists for online services, a denial of service mitigation service is used.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-g3 - Security Principle: G3
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-g3_smt}} - The confidentiality, integrity and availability requirements for systems, applications and data are determined and documented.

securityControls.title: ism-1175 - Control: ism-1175
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1175_smt}} - Privileged user accounts are prevented from accessing the internet, email and web services.

securityControls.title: ism-1431 - Control: ism-1431
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1431_smt}} - Denial-of-service attack mitigation strategies are discussed with cloud service providers, specifically: * their capacity to withstand denial-of-service attacks * any costs likely to be incurred as a result of denial-of-service attacks * thresholds for notification of denial-of-service attacks * thresholds for turning off online services during denial-of-service attacks * pre-approved actions that can be undertaken during denial-of-service attacks * any arrangements with upstream service providers to block malicious network traffic as far upstream as possible.

securityControls.title: ism-0428 - Control: ism-0428
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0428_smt}} - Systems are configured with a session or screen lock that: * activates after a maximum of 15 minutes of user inactivity, or if manually activated by users * conceals all session content on the screen * ensures that the screen does not enter a power saving state before the session or screen lock is activated * requires users to authenticate to unlock the session * denies users the ability to disable the session or screen locking mechanism.

securityControls.title: ism-1792 - Control: ism-1792
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1792_smt}} - The authenticity of applications, ICT equipment and services are assessed as part of acceptance of products and services.

securityControls.title: ism-1322 - Control: ism-1322
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1322_smt}} - Evaluated supplicants, authenticators, wireless access points and authentication servers are used in wireless networks.

securityControls.title: ism-0375 - Control: ism-0375
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0375_smt}} - Following sanitisation, destruction or declassification, a formal administrative decision is made to release media, or its waste, into the public domain.

securityControls.title: ism-0415 - Control: ism-0415
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0415_smt}} - The use of shared user accounts is strictly controlled, and personnel using such accounts are uniquely identifiable.

securityControls.title: ism-0888 - Control: ism-0888
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0888_smt}} - Security documentation is reviewed at least annually and includes a ‘current as at \[date\]’ or equivalent statement.

securityControls.title: ism-1523 - Control: ism-1523
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1523_smt}} - A sample of security-relevant events relating to data transfer policies are taken at least every 3 months and assessed against security policies for CDSs to identify any operational failures.

securityControls.title: ism-1269 - Control: ism-1269
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1269_smt}} - Database servers and web servers are functionally separated.

securityControls.title: ism-1522 - Control: ism-1522
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1522_smt}} - CDSs implement independent security-enforcing functions for upward and downward network paths.

securityControls.title: ism-0556 - Control: ism-0556
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0556_smt}} - Workstations are not connected to video conferencing units or IP phones unless the workstation or the device uses Virtual Local Area Networks or similar mechanisms to maintain separation between video conferencing, IP telephony and other data traffic.

securityControls.title: ism-1739 - Control: ism-1739
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1739_smt}} - A system’s security architecture is approved prior to the development of the system.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p7 - Security Principle: P7
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p7_smt}} - Data is encrypted at rest and in transit between different systems.

securityControls.title: ism-0611 - Control: ism-0611
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0611_smt}} - System administrators for gateways are assigned the minimum privileges required to perform their duties.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p6 - Security Principle: P6
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p6_smt}} - Only trusted and supported operating systems, applications and computer code can execute on systems.

securityControls.title: ism-1593 - Control: ism-1593
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1593_smt}} - Users provide sufficient evidence to verify their identity when requesting new credentials.

securityControls.title: ism-1646 - Control: ism-1646
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1646_smt}} - Floor plan diagrams contain the following: * cable paths (including ingress and egress points between floors) * cable reticulation system and conduit paths * floor concentration boxes * wall outlet boxes * network cabinets.

securityControls.title: ism-1107 - Control: ism-1107
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1107_smt}} - OFFICIAL and PROTECTED wall outlet boxes are coloured neither salmon pink nor red.

securityControls.title: ism-0931 - Control: ism-0931
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0931_smt}} - In SECRET and TOP SECRET areas, push-to-talk handsets or push-to-talk headsets are used to meet any off-hook audio protection requirements.

securityControls.title: ism-1323 - Control: ism-1323
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1323_smt}} - Certificates are required for both devices and users accessing wireless networks.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p11 - Security Principle: P11
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p11_smt}} - Personnel are granted the minimum access to systems, applications and data repositories required for their duties.

securityControls.title: ism-1059 - Control: ism-1059
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1059_smt}} - All data stored on media is encrypted.

securityControls.title: ism-1083 - Control: ism-1083
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1083_smt}} - Personnel are advised of the sensitivity or classification permitted for voice and data communications when using mobile devices.

securityControls.title: ism-1609 - Control: ism-1609
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1609_smt}} - System owners are consulted before allowing intrusion activity to continue on a system for the purpose of collecting further data or evidence.

securityControls.title: ism-1797 - Control: ism-1797
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1797_smt}} - Installers, patches and updates are digitally signed or provided with cryptographic checksums as part of application development.

securityControls.title: ism-0810 - Control: ism-0810
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0810_smt}} - Systems are secured in facilities that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their sensitivity or classification.

securityControls.title: ism-1626 - Control: ism-1626
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1626_smt}} - Legal advice is sought regarding the development and implementation of a trusted insider program.

securityControls.title: ism-0321 - Control: ism-0321
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0321_smt}} - When disposing of ICT equipment that has been designed or modified to meet emanation security standards, the ACSC is contacted for requirements relating to its disposal.

securityControls.title: ism-0294 - Control: ism-0294
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0294_smt}} - ICT equipment, with the exception of high assurance ICT equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.

securityControls.title: ism-0612 - Control: ism-0612
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0612_smt}} - System administrators for gateways are formally trained on the operation and management of gateways.

securityControls.title: ism-1296 - Control: ism-1296
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1296_smt}} - Physical security is implemented to protect network devices in public areas from physical damage or unauthorised access.

securityControls.title: ism-0362 - Control: ism-0362
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0362_smt}} - Product-specific directions provided by degausser manufacturers are followed.

securityControls.title: ism-1470 - Control: ism-1470
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1470_smt}} - Unneeded components, services and functionality of office productivity suites, web browsers, email clients, PDF software and security products are disabled or removed.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p14 - Security Principle: P14
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-p14_smt}} - Physical access to systems, supporting infrastructure and facilities is restricted to authorised personnel.

securityControls.title: ism-1716 - Control: ism-1716
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1716_smt}} - Access to data repositories is automatically disabled after 45 days of inactivity.

securityControls.title: ism-1794 - Control: ism-1794
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1794_smt}} - A minimum notification period of one month by service providers for significant changes to their own service provider arrangements is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

securityControls.title: ism-0133 - Control: ism-0133
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0133_smt}} - When a data spill occurs, data owners are advised and access to the data is restricted.

securityControls.title: ism-1584 - Control: ism-1584
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1584_smt}} - Unprivileged users are prevented from bypassing, disabling or modifying security functionality of operating systems.

securityControls.title: ism-0724 - Control: ism-0724
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0724_smt}} - The CISO implements cyber security measurement metrics and key performance indicators for their organisation.

securityControls.title: ism-0447 - Control: ism-0447
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0447_smt}} - Foreign nationals, excluding seconded foreign nationals, do not have privileged access to systems that process, store or communicate AGAO data.

securityControls.title: ism-0216 - Control: ism-0216
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0216_smt}} - TOP SECRET and non-TOP SECRET patch panels are physically separated by installing them in separate cabinets.

securityControls.title: ism-1559 - Control: ism-1559
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1559_smt}} - Memorised secrets used for multi-factor authentication are a minimum of 6 characters, unless more stringent requirements apply.

securityControls.title: ism-1053 - Control: ism-1053
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1053_smt}} - Servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in server rooms or communications rooms that meet the requirements for a security zone suitable for their sensitivity or classification.

securityControls.title: ism-1782 - Control: ism-1782
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1782_smt}} - A protective DNS service is used to block access to known malicious domain names.

securityControls.title: ism-1103 - Control: ism-1103
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1103_smt}} - In TOP SECRET areas, cable reticulation systems leading into cabinets not in server rooms or communications rooms are terminated at the boundary of the cabinet.

securityControls.title: ism-1095 - Control: ism-1095
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1095_smt}} - Wall outlet boxes denote the systems, cable identifiers and wall outlet box identifier.

securityControls.title: ism-1693 - Control: ism-1693
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1693_smt}} - Patches, updates or vendor mitigations for security vulnerabilities in other applications are applied within one month of release.

securityControls.title: ism-1130 - Control: ism-1130
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1130_smt}} - In shared facilities, cables are run in an enclosed cable reticulation system.

securityControls.title: ism-0311 - Control: ism-0311
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0311_smt}} - ICT equipment containing media is sanitised by removing the media from the ICT equipment or by sanitising the media in situ.

securityControls.title: ism-0829 - Control: ism-0829
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0829_smt}} - Security measures are used to detect and respond to unauthorised RF devices in SECRET and TOP SECRET areas.

securityControls.title: ism-1533 - Control: ism-1533
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1533_smt}} - A mobile device management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1591 - Control: ism-1591
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1591_smt}} - Access to systems, applications and data repositories is removed or suspended as soon as practicable when personnel are detected undertaking malicious activities.

securityControls.title: ism-1686 - Control: ism-1686
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1686_smt}} - Windows Defender Credential Guard and Windows Defender Remote Credential Guard are enabled.

securityControls.title: ism-1526 - Control: ism-1526
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1526_smt}} - System owners monitor each system, and associated cyber threats, security risks and controls, on an ongoing basis.

securityControls.title: ism-1417 - Control: ism-1417
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1417_smt}} - Antivirus software is implemented on workstations and servers with: * signature-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level * heuristic-based detection functionality enabled and set to a high level * reputation rating functionality enabled * ransomware protection functionality enabled * detection signatures configured to update on at least a daily basis * regular scanning configured for all fixed disks and removable media.

securityControls.title: ism-1635 - Control: ism-1635
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1635_smt}} - System owners implement controls for each system and its operating environment.

securityControls.title: ism-0336 - Control: ism-0336
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0336_smt}} - An ICT equipment register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

securityControls.title: ism-1098 - Control: ism-1098
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1098_smt}} - Cables are terminated in individual cabinets; or for small systems, one cabinet with a division plate to delineate cable groups.

securityControls.title: ism-1525 - Control: ism-1525
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1525_smt}} - System owners register each system with its authorising officer.

securityControls.title: ism-0991 - Control: ism-0991
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0991_smt}} - Domain Name System and web proxy event logs are retained for at least 18 months.

securityControls.title: ism-1392 - Control: ism-1392
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1392_smt}} - When implementing application control using path rules, only approved users can write to and modify content within approved folders and files.

securityControls.title: ism-0306 - Control: ism-0306
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0306_smt}} - If an uncleared technician is used to undertake maintenance or repairs of ICT equipment, the technician is escorted by someone who: * is appropriately cleared and briefed * takes due care to ensure that data is not disclosed * takes all responsible measures to ensure the integrity of the ICT equipment * has the authority to direct the technician * is sufficiently familiar with the ICT equipment to understand the work being performed.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-g5 - Security Principle: G5
securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-g5_smt}} - Security risks are identified, documented, managed and accepted both before systems and applications are authorised for use, and continuously throughout their operational life.

securityControls.title: ism-1123 - Control: ism-1123
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1123_smt}} - A power distribution board with a feed from an Uninterruptible Power Supply is used to power all TOP SECRET ICT equipment.

securityControls.title: ism-0494 - Control: ism-0494
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0494_smt}} - Tunnel mode is used for IPsec connections; however, if using transport mode, an IP tunnel is used.

securityControls.title: ism-0569 - Control: ism-0569
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0569_smt}} - Emails are routed via centralised email gateways.

securityControls.title: ism-0836 - Control: ism-0836
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0836_smt}} - Non-volatile EEPROM media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

securityControls.title: ism-1088 - Control: ism-1088
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1088_smt}} - Personnel report the potential compromise of mobile devices, removable media or credentials to their organisation as soon as possible, especially if they: * provide credentials to foreign government officials * decrypt mobile devices for foreign government officials * have mobile devices taken out of sight by foreign government officials * have mobile devices or removable media stolen that are later returned * lose mobile devices or removable media that are later found * observe unusual behaviour of mobile devices.

securityControls.title: ism-1075 - Control: ism-1075
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1075_smt}} - The sender of a fax message makes arrangements for the receiver to collect the fax message as soon as possible after it is sent and for the receiver to notify the sender if the fax message does not arrive in an agreed amount of time.

securityControls.title: ism-0821 - Control: ism-0821
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0821_smt}} - Personnel are advised of security risks associated with posting personal information to online services and are encouraged to use any available privacy settings to restrict who can view such information.

securityControls.title: ism-0245 - Control: ism-0245
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0245_smt}} - A direct connection from an MFD to a digital telephone system is not enabled unless the digital telephone system is authorised to operate at the same sensitivity or classification as the network to which the MFD is connected.

securityControls.title: ism-1508 - Control: ism-1508
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1508_smt}} - Privileged access to systems and applications is limited to only what is required for users and services to undertake their duties.

securityControls.title: ism-1808 - Control: ism-1808
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1808_smt}} - A vulnerability scanner with an up-to-date vulnerability database is used for vulnerability scanning activities.

securityControls.title: ism-0411 - Control: ism-0411
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0411_smt}} - Foreign nationals, excluding seconded foreign nationals, do not have access to systems that process, store or communicate AGAO data unless effective controls are in place to ensure such data is not accessible to them.

securityControls.title: ism-1182 - Control: ism-1182
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1182_smt}} - Network access controls are implemented to limit network traffic within and between network segments to only those required for business purposes.

securityControls.title: ism-0310 - Control: ism-0310
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0310_smt}} - ICT equipment maintained or repaired off site is done so at facilities approved for handling the sensitivity or classification of the ICT equipment.

securityControls.title: ism-1461 - Control: ism-1461
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1461_smt}} - When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server’s hardware for SECRET or TOP SECRET computing environments, the physical server and all computing environments are of the same classification and belong to the same security domain.

securityControls.title: ism-1602 - Control: ism-1602
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1602_smt}} - Security documentation, including notification of subsequent changes, is communicated to all stakeholders.

securityControls.title: ism-1241 - Control: ism-1241
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1241_smt}} - Output encoding is performed on all output produced by web applications.

securityControls.title: ism-1649 - Control: ism-1649
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1649_smt}} - Just-in-time administration is used for administering systems and applications.

securityControls.title: ism-1745 - Control: ism-1745
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1745_smt}} - Early Launch Antimalware, Secure Boot, Trusted Boot and Measured Boot functionality is enabled.

securityControls.title: ism-0312 - Control: ism-0312
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0312_smt}} - ICT equipment, including associated media, that is located overseas and has processed, stored or communicated AUSTEO or AGAO data that cannot be sanitised in situ, is returned to Australia for destruction.

securityControls.title: ism-0735 - Control: ism-0735
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0735_smt}} - The CISO oversees the development, implementation and maintenance of their organisation’s cyber security awareness training program.

securityControls.title: ism-0230 - Control: ism-0230
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0230_smt}} - Personnel are advised of security risks posed by non-secure telephone systems in areas where sensitive or classified conversations can occur.

securityControls.title: ism-1657 - Control: ism-1657
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1657_smt}} - Application control restricts the execution of executables, software libraries, scripts, installers, compiled HTML, HTML applications and control panel applets to an organisation-approved set.

securityControls.title: ism-1091 - Control: ism-1091
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1091_smt}} - Keying material is changed when compromised or suspected of being compromised.

securityControls.title: ism-0549 - Control: ism-0549
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0549_smt}} - Video conferencing and IP telephony traffic is separated physically or logically from other data traffic.

securityControls.title: ism-0354 - Control: ism-0354
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0354_smt}} - Non-volatile magnetic media is sanitised by overwriting it at least once (or three times if pre-2001 or under 15 GB) in its entirety with a random pattern followed by a read back for verification.

securityControls.title: ism-1488 - Control: ism-1488
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1488_smt}} - Microsoft Office macros in files originating from the internet are blocked.

securityControls.title: ism-1786 - Control: ism-1786
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1786_smt}} - An approved supplier list is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-0555 - Control: ism-0555
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0555_smt}} - Authentication and authorisation is used for all actions on an IP telephony network, including registering a new IP phone, changing phone users, changing settings and accessing voicemail.

securityControls.title: ism-1658 - Control: ism-1658
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1658_smt}} - Application control restricts the execution of drivers to an organisation-approved set.

securityControls.title: ism-1247 - Control: ism-1247
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1247_smt}} - Unneeded accounts, components, services and functionality of DBMS software are disabled or removed.

securityControls.title: ism-1537 - Control: ism-1537
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1537_smt}} - The following events are logged for databases: * access or modification of particularly important content * addition of new users, especially privileged users * changes to user roles or privileges * attempts to elevate user privileges * queries containing comments * queries containing multiple embedded queries * database and query alerts or failures * database structure changes * database administrator actions * use of executable commands * database logons and logoffs.

securityControls.title: ism-1659 - Control: ism-1659
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1659_smt}} - Microsoft’s ‘recommended driver block rules’ are implemented.

securityControls.title: ism-1553 - Control: ism-1553
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1553_smt}} - TLS compression is disabled for TLS connections.

securityControls.title: ism-1720 - Control: ism-1720
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1720_smt}} - SECRET wall outlet boxes are coloured salmon pink.

securityControls.title: ism-1670 - Control: ism-1670

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1784_smt}} - The incident management policy, including the associated incident response plan, is exercised at least annually.

securityControls.title: ism-1460 - Control: ism-1460
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1460_smt}} - When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server’s hardware, the isolation mechanism is from a vendor that has made a commitment to secure-by-design principles, secure programming practices and maintaining the security of their products.

securityControls.title: ism-1714 - Control: ism-1714
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1714_smt}} - Unprivileged access event logs are stored centrally.

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p12 - Security Principle: P12

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1604_smt}} - When using a software-based isolation mechanism to share a physical server’s hardware, the configuration of the isolation mechanism is hardened by removing unneeded functionality and restricting access to the administrative interface used to manage the isolation mechanism.

securityControls.title: ism-1013 - Control: ism-1013
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1013_smt}} - The effective range of wireless communications outside an organisation’s area of control is limited by implementing RF shielding on facilities in which SECRET or TOP SECRET wireless networks are used.

securityControls.title: ism-1548 - Control: ism-1548

securityControls.title: ism-0430 - Control: ism-0430

securityControls.title: ism-1585 - Control: ism-1585

securityControls.title: ism-0613 - Control: ism-0613

securityControls.title: ism-1412 - Control: ism-1412
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1412_smt}} - ACSC or vendor hardening guidance for web browsers, Microsoft Office and PDF software is implemented.

securityControls.title: ism-1596 - Control: ism-1596

securityControls.title: ism-1624 - Control: ism-1624

securityControls.title: ism-1615 - Control: ism-1615
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1615_smt}} - Break glass accounts are tested after credentials are changed.

securityControls.title: ism-1071 - Control: ism-1071

securityControls.title: ism-1785 - Control: ism-1785
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1785_smt}} - A supplier relationship management policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1576 - Control: ism-1576

securityControls.title: ism-0554 - Control: ism-0554

securityControls.title: ism-1380 - Control: ism-1380

securityControls.description: • {{ism-0027_smt}} - System owners obtain authorisation to operate each system from its authorising officer based on the acceptance of the security risks associated with its operation.

securityControls.title: ism-1467 - Control: ism-1467
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1467_smt}} - The latest release of office productivity suites, web browsers and their extensions, email clients, PDF software, and security products are used.

securityControls.title: ism-1271 - Control: ism-1271

securityControls.title: ism-1651 - Control: ism-1651
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1651_smt}} - Privileged access event logs are stored centrally.

securityControls.title: ism-1037 - Control: ism-1037
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1037_smt}} - Gateways undergo testing following configuration changes, and at regular intervals no more than six months apart, to validate they conform to expected security configurations.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1501_smt}} - Operating systems that are no longer supported by vendors are replaced.

securityControls.title: ism-1789 - Control: ism-1789
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1789_smt}} - Sufficient spares of critical ICT equipment are sourced and kept in reserve.

securityControls.title: ism-0863 - Control: ism-0863
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0863_smt}} - Mobile devices prevent personnel from installing or uninstalling non-approved applications once provisioned.

securityControls.title: ism-0576 - Control: ism-0576
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0576_smt}} - An incident management policy, and associated incident response plan, is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1634 - Control: ism-1634

securityControls.title: ism-0137 - Control: ism-0137
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0137_smt}} - Legal advice is sought before allowing intrusion activity to continue on a system for the purpose of collecting further data or evidence.

securityControls.title: ism-1332 - Control: ism-1332
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1332_smt}} - WPA3-Enterprise 192-bit mode is used to protect the confidentiality and integrity of all wireless network traffic.

securityControls.title: ism-0590 - Control: ism-0590
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0590_smt}} - Controls for MFDs connected to networks are of a similar strength to those for other devices on networks.

securityControls.title: ism-1478 - Control: ism-1478
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1478_smt}} - The CISO oversees their organisation’s cyber security program and ensures their organisation’s compliance with cyber security policy, standards, regulations and legislation.

securityControls.title: ism-0260 - Control: ism-0260
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0260_smt}} - All web access, including that by internal servers, is conducted through web proxies.

securityControls.title: ism-1791 - Control: ism-1791
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1791_smt}} - The integrity of applications, ICT equipment and services are assessed as part of acceptance of products and services.

securityControls.title: ism-1146 - Control: ism-1146
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1146_smt}} - Personnel are advised to maintain separate work and personal accounts for online services.

securityControls.title: ism-1788 - Control: ism-1788
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1788_smt}} - Multiple potential suppliers are identified for sourcing critical applications, ICT equipment and services.

securityControls.title: ism-0332 - Control: ism-0332
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0332_smt}} - Media, with the exception of internally mounted fixed media within ICT equipment, is labelled with protective markings reflecting its sensitivity or classification.

securityControls.title: ism-1809 - Control: ism-1809
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1809_smt}} - When applications, operating systems, network devices or other ICT equipment that are no longer supported by vendors cannot be immediately removed or replaced, compensating controls are implemented until such time that they can be removed or replaced.

securityControls.title: ism-0138 - Control: ism-0138

securityControls.title: ism-1572 - Control: ism-1572

securityControls.title: ism-1754 - Control: ism-1754
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1754_smt}} - Security vulnerabilities identified in applications are resolved by software developers.

securityControls.title: ism-0078 - Control: ism-0078

securityControls.title: ism-1703 - Control: ism-1703

securityControls.title: ism-principle-p8 - Security Principle: P8

securityControls.title: ism-0861 - Control: ism-0861
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0861_smt}} - DKIM signing is enabled on emails originating from an organisation’s domains (including subdomains).

securityControls.title: ism-0211 - Control: ism-0211

securityControls.title: ism-1122 - Control: ism-1122
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1122_smt}} - Where wall penetrations exit a TOP SECRET area into a lower classified area, TOP SECRET cables are encased in conduit with all gaps between the TOP SECRET conduit and the wall filled with an appropriate sealing compound.

securityControls.title: ism-1065 - Control: ism-1065

securityControls.title: ism-1747 - Control: ism-1747
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1747_smt}} - Operating system event logs are stored centrally.

securityControls.title: ism-0441 - Control: ism-0441
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0441_smt}} - When personnel are granted temporary access to a system, effective controls are put in place to restrict their access to only data required for them to undertake their duties.

securityControls.title: ism-1631 - Control: ism-1631
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1631_smt}} - Suppliers of applications, ICT equipment and services associated with systems are identified.

securityControls.title: ism-1642 - Control: ism-1642
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1642_smt}} - Media is sanitised before it is reused in a different security domain.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1491_smt}} - Unprivileged users are prevented from running script execution engines, including: * Windows Script Host (cscript.exe and wscript.exe) * PowerShell (powershell.exe, powershell_ise.exe and pwsh.exe) * Command Prompt (cmd.exe) * Windows Management Instrumentation (wmic.exe) * Microsoft Hypertext Markup Language (HTML) Application Host (mshta.exe).

securityControls.title: ism-1111 - Control: ism-1111

securityControls.title: ism-0409 - Control: ism-0409

securityControls.description: • {{ism-0195_smt}} - In shared facilities, uniquely identifiable SCEC-approved tamper-evident seals are used to seal all removable covers on TOP SECRET cable reticulation systems.

securityControls.title: ism-1653 - Control: ism-1653

securityControls.title: ism-0820 - Control: ism-0820
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0820_smt}} - Personnel are advised to not post work information to unauthorised online services and to report cases where such information is posted.

securityControls.title: ism-0229 - Control: ism-0229
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0229_smt}} - Personnel are advised of the permitted sensitivity or classification of information that can be discussed over both internal and external telephone systems.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-principle-r2_smt}} - Cyber security incidents are contained, eradicated and recovered from in a timely manner.

securityControls.title: ism-0732 - Control: ism-0732
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0732_smt}} - The CISO receives and manages a dedicated cyber security budget for their organisation.

securityControls.title: ism-1520 - Control: ism-1520
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1520_smt}} - System administrators for gateways undergo appropriate employment screening and, where necessary, hold an appropriate security clearance based on the sensitivity or classification of gateways.

securityControls.title: ism-1625 - Control: ism-1625
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1625_smt}} - A trusted insider program is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1446 - Control: ism-1446
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1446_smt}} - When using elliptic curve cryptography, a curve from FIPS 186-4 is used.

securityControls.title: ism-1583 - Control: ism-1583
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1583_smt}} - Personnel who are contractors are identified as such.

securityControls.title: ism-0874 - Control: ism-0874
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0874_smt}} - Mobile devices access the internet via a VPN connection to an organisation’s internet gateway rather than via a direct connection to the internet.

securityControls.title: ism-1089 - Control: ism-1089
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1089_smt}} - Protective marking tools do not allow users replying to or forwarding emails to select protective markings lower than previously used.

securityControls.title: ism-1648 - Control: ism-1648

securityControls.title: ism-1637 - Control: ism-1637
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1637_smt}} - An outsourced cloud service register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

securityControls.title: ism-1574 - Control: ism-1574
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1574_smt}} - The storage of data in a portable manner that allows for backups, service migration and service decommissioning without any loss of data is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

securityControls.title: ism-1255 - Control: ism-1255
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1255_smt}} - Database users’ ability to access, insert, modify and remove database contents is restricted based on their work duties.

securityControls.title: ism-1400 - Control: ism-1400
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1400_smt}} - Personnel accessing OFFICIAL and PROTECTED systems or data using a privately-owned mobile device use an ASD-approved platform, a security configuration in accordance with ACSC guidance, and have enforced separation of work and personal data.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1570_smt}} - Outsourced cloud service providers and their cloud services undergo a security assessment by an IRAP assessor at least every 24 months.

securityControls.title: ism-1014 - Control: ism-1014
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1014_smt}} - Individual logins are implemented for IP phones used for SECRET or TOP SECRET conversations.

securityControls.title: ism-1234 - Control: ism-1234
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1234_smt}} - Email content filtering is implemented to filter potentially harmful content in email bodies and attachments.

securityControls.title: ism-0717 - Control: ism-0717
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0717_smt}} - The CISO oversees the management of cyber security personnel within their organisation.

securityControls.title: ism-0588 - Control: ism-0588
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0588_smt}} - A fax machine and MFD usage policy is developed, implemented and maintained.

securityControls.title: ism-1486 - Control: ism-1486
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1486_smt}} - Web browsers do not process Java from the internet.

securityControls.title: ism-1027 - Control: ism-1027
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1027_smt}} - Email distribution list software used by external senders is configured such that it does not break the validity of the sender’s DKIM signature.

securityControls.title: ism-1076 - Control: ism-1076
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1076_smt}} - Televisions and computer monitors with minor burn-in or image persistence are sanitised by displaying a solid white image on the screen for an extended period of time.

securityControls.title: ism-1805 - Control: ism-1805
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1805_smt}} - A denial of service response plan for video conferencing and IP telephony services contains the following: * how to identify signs of a denial-of-service attack * how to identify the source of a denial-of-service attack * how capabilities can be maintained during a denial-of-service attack * what actions can be taken to respond to a denial-of-service attack.

securityControls.title: ism-1200 - Control: ism-1200
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1200_smt}} - Bluetooth pairing for OFFICIAL and PROTECTED mobile devices is performed using Secure Connections, preferably with Numeric Comparison if supported.

securityControls.title: ism-1726 - Control: ism-1726
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1726_smt}} - Optical disks are destroyed using a furnace/incinerator, hammer mill, disintegrator, grinder/sander or by cutting.

securityControls.title: ism-1213 - Control: ism-1213
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1213_smt}} - Following intrusion remediation activities, full network traffic is captured for at least seven days and analysed to determine whether the adversary has been successfully removed from the system.

securityControls.title: ism-1530 - Control: ism-1530
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1530_smt}} - Servers, network devices and cryptographic equipment are secured in security containers or secure rooms suitable for their sensitivity or classification taking into account the combination of security zones they reside in.

securityControls.title: ism-1096 - Control: ism-1096
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1096_smt}} - Cables are labelled at each end with sufficient source and destination details to enable the physical identification and inspection of the cable.

securityControls.title: ism-0405 - Control: ism-0405
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0405_smt}} - Requests for unprivileged access to systems, applications and data repositories are validated when first requested.

securityControls.title: ism-1702 - Control: ism-1702
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1702_smt}} - A vulnerability scanner is used at least weekly to identify missing patches or updates for security vulnerabilities in operating systems of workstations, servers and network devices.

securityControls.title: ism-1036 - Control: ism-1036
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1036_smt}} - Fax machines and MFDs are located in areas where their use can be observed.

securityControls.title: ism-1736 - Control: ism-1736
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1736_smt}} - A managed service register is developed, implemented, maintained and verified on a regular basis.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1556_smt}} - If returning from travelling overseas with mobile devices to high or extreme risk countries, personnel take the following additional actions: * reset credentials used with mobile devices, including those used for remote access to their organisation’s systems * monitor accounts for any indicators of compromise, such as failed logon attempts.

securityControls.title: ism-0140 - Control: ism-0140
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0140_smt}} - Cyber security incidents are reported to the ACSC.

securityControls.title: ism-1435 - Control: ism-1435

securityControls.title: ism-1611 - Control: ism-1611
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1611_smt}} - Break glass accounts are only used when normal authentication processes cannot be used.

securityControls.title: ism-0553 - Control: ism-0553
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0553_smt}} - Authentication and authorisation is used for all actions on a video conferencing network, including call setup and changing settings.

securityControls.title: ism-1569 - Control: ism-1569
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1569_smt}} - A shared responsibility model is created, documented and shared between suppliers and their customers in order to articulate the security responsibilities of each party.

securityControls.title: ism-0455 - Control: ism-0455
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0455_smt}} - Where practical, cryptographic equipment and software provides a means of data recovery to allow for circumstances where the encryption key is unavailable due to loss, damage or failure.

securityControls.title: ism-1451 - Control: ism-1451
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1451_smt}} - Types of data and its ownership is documented in contractual arrangements with service providers.

securityControls.title: ism-0201 - Control: ism-0201
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0201_smt}} - Labels for TOP SECRET conduits are a minimum size of 2.5 cm x 1 cm, attached at five-metre intervals and marked as ‘TS RUN’.

securityControls.title: ism-0407 - Control: ism-0407

securityControls.title: ism-1504 - Control: ism-1504

securityControls.title: ism-1237 - Control: ism-1237

securityControls.title: ism-1697 - Control: ism-1697

securityControls.title: ism-0350 - Control: ism-0350
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0350_smt}} - The following media types are destroyed prior to their disposal: * microfiche and microfilm * optical discs * programmable read-only memory * read-only memory * other types of media that cannot be sanitised.

securityControls.title: ism-1023 - Control: ism-1023
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1023_smt}} - The intended recipients of blocked inbound emails, and the senders of blocked outbound emails, are notified.

securityControls.title: ism-1608 - Control: ism-1608

securityControls.title: ism-1555 - Control: ism-1555
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1555_smt}} - Before travelling overseas with mobile devices, personnel take the following actions: * record all details of the mobile devices being taken, such as product types, serial numbers and International Mobile Equipment Identity numbers * update all operating systems and applications * remove all non-essential accounts, applications and data * apply security configuration settings, such as lock screens * configure remote locate and wipe functionality * enable encryption, including for any removable media * backup all important data and configuration settings.

securityControls.title: ism-0380 - Control: ism-0380
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0380_smt}} - Unneeded accounts, components, services and functionality of operating systems are disabled or removed.

securityControls.title: ism-1748 - Control: ism-1748

securityControls.title: ism-1359 - Control: ism-1359

securityControls.title: ism-1613 - Control: ism-1613

securityControls.title: ism-0869 - Control: ism-0869

securityControls.title: ism-1722 - Control: ism-1722

securityControls.title: ism-1671 - Control: ism-1671

securityControls.title: ism-0269 - Control: ism-0269

securityControls.title: ism-1114 - Control: ism-1114

securityControls.title: ism-1644 - Control: ism-1644
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1644_smt}} - Sensitive or classified phone calls are not conducted in public locations unless care is taken to reduce the chance of conversations being overheard.

securityControls.title: ism-1682 - Control: ism-1682

securityControls.title: ism-1575 - Control: ism-1575

securityControls.title: ism-1220 - Control: ism-1220
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1220_smt}} - Printer and MFD platens are inspected and destroyed if any text or images are retained on the platen.

securityControls.title: ism-0551 - Control: ism-0551
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0551_smt}} - IP telephony is configured such that: * IP phones authenticate themselves to the call controller upon registration * auto-registration is disabled and only authorised devices are allowed to access the network * unauthorised devices are blocked by default * all unused and prohibited functionality is disabled.

securityControls.title: ism-0547 - Control: ism-0547

securityControls.title: ism-0835 - Control: ism-0835

securityControls.title: ism-0570 - Control: ism-0570

securityControls.title: ism-1654 - Control: ism-1654

securityControls.title: ism-0316 - Control: ism-0316

securityControls.title: ism-1603 - Control: ism-1603
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1603_smt}} - Authentication methods susceptible to replay attacks are disabled.

securityControls.title: ism-0373 - Control: ism-0373
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0373_smt}} - Personnel supervising the destruction of media storing accountable material supervise its handling to the point of destruction, ensure that the destruction is completed successfully and sign a destruction certificate afterwards.

securityControls.title: ism-1676 - Control: ism-1676
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1676_smt}} - Microsoft Office’s list of trusted publishers is validated on an annual or more frequent basis.

securityControls.title: ism-0371 - Control: ism-0371

securityControls.title: ism-0280 - Control: ism-0280

securityControls.title: ism-0585 - Control: ism-0585

securityControls.title: ism-0240 - Control: ism-0240

securityControls.title: ism-1429 - Control: ism-1429

securityControls.title: ism-1510 - Control: ism-1510

securityControls.title: ism-0864 - Control: ism-0864

securityControls.title: ism-1711 - Control: ism-1711

securityControls.title: ism-0687 - Control: ism-0687

securityControls.title: ism-0393 - Control: ism-0393

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1471_smt}} - When implementing application control using publisher certificate rules, both publisher names and product names are used.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-0694_smt}} - Privately-owned mobile devices do not access SECRET and TOP SECRET systems or data.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1387_smt}} - Administrative activities are conducted through jump servers.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1487_smt}} - Only privileged users responsible for validating that Microsoft Office macros are free of malicious code can write to and modify content within Trusted Locations.

securityControls.description: • {{ism-1589_smt}} - MTA-STS is enabled to prevent the unencrypted transfer of emails between complying servers.

securityControls.title: ism-0417 - Control: ism-0417
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0417_smt}} - When systems cannot support multi-factor authentication, single-factor authentication using passphrases is implemented instead.

securityControls.title: ism-0164 - Control: ism-0164
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0164_smt}} - Unauthorised people are prevented from observing systems, in particular workstation displays and keyboards, within facilities.

securityControls.title: ism-1299 - Control: ism-1299
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1299_smt}} - Personnel take the following precautions when travelling overseas with mobile devices: * never leaving mobile devices or removable media unattended for any period of time, including by placing them in checked-in luggage or leaving them in hotel safes * never storing credentials with mobile devices that they grant access to, such as in laptop bags * never lending mobile devices or removable media to untrusted people, even if briefly * never allowing untrusted people to connect their mobile devices or removable media, including for charging * never using designated charging stations, wall outlet charging ports or chargers supplied by untrusted people * avoiding connecting mobile devices to open or untrusted Wi-Fi networks * using a VPN connection to encrypt all mobile device communications * using encrypted messaging apps for communications instead of using foreign telecommunication networks * disabling any communications capabilities of mobile devices when not in use, such as cellular data, wireless, Bluetooth and Near Field Communication * avoiding reuse of removable media once used with other parties’ systems or mobile devices * ensuring any removable media used for data transfers are thoroughly checked for malicious code beforehand * never using any gifted mobile devices, especially removable media, when travelling or upon returning from travelling.

securityControls.title: ism-1448 - Control: ism-1448

securityControls.title: ism-0198 - Control: ism-0198
securityControls.description: • {{ism-0198_smt}} - When penetrating a TOP SECRET audio secure room, the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation is consulted and all directions provided are complied with.

securityControls.title: ism-1517 - Control: ism-1517
securityControls.description: • {{ism-1517_smt}} - Equipment that is capable of reducing microform to a fine powder, with resultant particles not showing more than five consecutive characters per particle upon microscopic inspection, is used to destroy microfiche and microfilm.
